tools.description: Resolve a work email → LinkedIn profile URL. Highest-accuracy URL resolver.

tools.name: find_website_by_company_name

tools.name: google_search_scraper
tools.description: Google SERP scraper — organic + featured results for a query.

tools.name: google_maps_places_scraper
tools.description: Scrape Google Maps Places by keyword + location — local-business prospecting and TAM sizing.

tools.name: google_maps_places_scraper_url
tools.description: Scrape places from a hand-crafted Google Maps URL — preserves UI-applied filters.

tools.name: google_maps_reviews_scraper
tools.description: Scrape Google Maps reviews for a place — VoC analysis and competitive review benchmarking.

discover_people.ask: Do I need breadth or precision?

breadth_cheap.note: Indexed dataset. Fastest + cheapest for broad discovery.

precision_live.note: Live LinkedIn string match. Good for exact name/title/company hits.

targeted_complex.note: 30+ filters, include+exclude, sessionId pagination. Use when cheap tools can't express the ICP.

account_based.note: When the target account is already known and you want every relevant contact at it.

batch.note: Async batch. Use for 10+ people. Follow with check_email_finding.

personal.note: Use only when work email is unavailable. Compliance/consent is your responsibility.

batch.note: Async, up to 1000. Follow with check_email_verification.

local_business.ask: Do you have a keyword + location, an existing Maps URL, or do you want reviews?

by_url.note: Use when the user hands you a custom Maps URL with applied filters.

reviews.note: VoC + reputation. Use sparingly on large places — review counts can be in the thousands.

linkedin_jobs.ask: Do you want a list, or full posting details?

list.note: Hiring-signal discovery. Returns posting metadata + jobIds.

detail.note: Full posting + description + apply URL. Always preceded by linkedin_job_search.

resolve_linkedin_url.ask: Do you have an email, or just name + company?

from_email.note: Highest accuracy. Always prefer when an email is available.

from_name.note: Common-name + large-company queries can be ambiguous. Provide company domain when possible.
